A great realistic fishing sim that is also user friendly to casual fishing fans.

You need to unwind from everyday struggle? Fed up with news about politics, wars, moderately intelligent football players and their plastic girls.. Well, this  game might be the exact medicine you need (after praying rosary of course)..     I am no expert, and sure I have a lot of hours, but what  I do is cast and chill.. Like that other game... Cast and Chill.. I have been casting a lot, and will continue to do so.. Making some pity cash, buying marginally better gear, having real excitement once I score proper trophy fish. Cooking, crafting, nothing too complicated, but still interesting..    Is it masterpiece? Of course not. It is a grind and endless game where player tries to figure out how to optimise the game and win the trophies. It is a Squid game for fish and relaxing unwind for player.    I recommend it to all people who need to vent out the fumes... Reducing stress for 250+ hours now.. It is a sort of therapy and I like it...    God bless guys, stay healthy stay humble and stay focused on Christ for he is the only way.    Love from Valentin.. See you on Russian lakes.. Send me baits, I accept gifts... :)

Listen, it can feel super grindy, even if you buy premium, but there is just something really satisfying about learning through experience (and quite a bit of time looking up stuff online). Once you commit to the idea of this game being (A) a simulator and (B) a fishing RPG and you just allow yourself to slow down and internalize those ideas, the game will eventually come to you if you just stick with it.     Tons of depth here, lots to do, lots to craft, and the fishing itself is great. The trees and fauna don't look great from afar, but up close they look good and, most importantly, the water looks GREAT! It's definitely a simulator, so expect to keep running into those "why can't I catch anything" type walls, but that just encourages you to seek help, try a different depth, lure, or branch out to try a different spot and discover the secrets of each lake.

GOOD:  Very enjoyable best-in-class fishing game/simulator, featuring extremely in-depth fishing and player status mechanics, vast equipment customization (clothing, rods, reels, hooks, bait, boats, etc.), beautifully made (albeit small) maps with unique animals and weather, a wide variety of highly detailed, realistic animal models, trophy lodges, competitive leaderboards, and more. The game features character levels which unlocks gear and other items, which can be purchased with the in-game currency. In-game currency is gained by completing tasks and or selling fish you've caught on the market. The game can be played in a variety of ways, from catching frogs with a cane pole and horse flies on the shallow bank of a pond to cruising out to the middle of the Norwegian sea and reeling in huge haddock.  BAD:  Multiplayer is possible via leaderboards and in-game item trading with other players, however it is currently NOT possible to join another players game or see the character models of other players. The game features a premium currency system and certain in-game items locked behind a pay wall. The graphics are mostly good with excellent lighting and reflections, but I do find myself wishing it was slightly better, especially when viewing the tree tops and leaves. Lastly, fish cannot be seen in the water unless actively hooked on your rod.

Russian Fishing 4 is a very realistic and rewarding fishing game. The mechanics are deep, the progression feels earned, and catching a rare fish actually feels exciting instead of scripted.    I have second account where I have played for 500 hours, and its very fun even after that much time. There is always something to grind for: better gear, new locations, or learning how different fish behave. The game doesn’t hold your hand, which makes success feel satisfying.    Graphics are simple but immersive, and the sound design is relaxing. It’s a great game if you enjoy patience, strategy, and long-term progression. Not for people who want fast action, but perfect if you like realistic simulation.    Overall, Russian Fishing 4 is one of the best fishing simulators out there, especially if you’re willing to invest time into it.

If you enjoy realistic fishing and don’t mind a slower-paced, thoughtful experience, this game is absolutely worth sinking hours into.  Rating: 9/10 – Highly recommended for fans of realistic fishing sims.

On the surface this is a cozy, relaxed fishing game where you can just sit back and catch fish. But after playing for just long enough to be introduced to the massive community this game has, you learn just how intricate and detailed everything is within the world of Russian Fishing 4. Line diameters matter, lure colors matter, water temperatures matter, weather matters, everything that can change about your equipment or environment can affect your fishing. If you are someone who loves to stress the details, and experiment with different strategies than what others take as the "standard", then this is a game you will love. The game balances progression and location together, keeping water bodies that would have fish too strong for your level locked away until you're ready. The micro transactions are not pushed heavily, and where they are pushed, they provide a significant boost without feeling cheaty or undervalued, if you have a steady income and are willing to spend money on pixels, you will not find much issue with these. The last part I would like to add is the trophy fish, the way this game rewards you finding the "correct" rig/lure/bait is fantastic, horns and bells and a big golden tag, along with unique models for the trophy sized fish is perfect!

TL;DR: Free to play, impossible to quit. Bring snacks, a second monitor for spreadsheets, and a therapist on speed dial.  RF4 reveiw: You're grinding silver coins like a crypto miner in Siberia, one minnow at a time. The Grind is real And Slightly Sadistic. There’s something darkly hilarious about spending four hours perfecting groundbait recipes just to land a 2kg bream that sells for less than a Starbucks latte.   Your $27,000 dream setup? One overzealous reel click and SNAP—there goes your line, your lure, and your will to live. Repair costs? Robbery.   Russian Fishing 4 isn’t a game. It’s a lifestyle. A cult. This game should include a support group for people who think “just one more cast” at 5 a.m. If you want instant gratification, go play Candy Crush. If you want to feel the soul-crushing joy of catching a fish worth less than the electricity you used to run your PC… welcome home.

If you can’t fish in real life — this is the second-best option.  The game only feels grindy if you focus on end-game gear. But if you let yourself progress gradually, as in real life, and simply enjoy the thrill of catching different species with capable equipment, you’ll have a great time.    P.S. For new players — the entry curve is simple, yet knowledge is king in this game. You’ll need to research small mechanics online (use guides). There are also many community groups sharing info about hot spots and current baits, since fish migrate and change preferences.    The system relies on RNG and statistics, so trial and error are necessary — no single formula works forever. Preparation is 70%, and the other 30% is pure luck.    Premium only helps with leveling — countless tests have proven there’s no difference in bite rates. However, unlocking a new waterbody without the right tackle is pointless. Take your time, research, chill, and enjoy.    This game isn’t meant to be beaten — it’s meant to be enjoyed.

More enjoyable than Fishing Planet. I played a bit of Fishing Planet and while that game is fun, it just felt annoying that you really need premium to play that game for any amount of time. And even then, you'll still get MTX pop ups constantly. Russian Fishing 4 on the other hand is more laid back with way cheaper travel costs, no license fees and no MTX pop ups, in fact the premium part of the game is fairly hidden.  The fishing physics feel more or less the same as Fishing Planet with RF4 being slightly more in depth. I thought the survival mechanics in this game would be annoying but it's really not an issue at all, it just ends up adding more to the game with the ability to cook your catches into various dishes. This game also looks way better if that's important to you.

This game is a money grab.   I spent 400 hours to get bottom fishing skill to 60% and another 1~2 weeks to reach 65%, only to discover that, in order to get bigger fish, I need to level it up to 100%. Not to mention that I also need to level up groundbait skill to 100% as well. So basically, from level 18~20 forward, you'll have to spend hundreds of hours grinding at the same boring spots over and over.  If you wanna know how the game works, It goes like this: - Enter the game and get a lot of fish, really fun. - Level up, get new gear, new maps and all. Still really fun. - Continue to level up but everything gets slower and repetitive the more you play, but you think you just need to level up a little bit more. - Level up a little bit more and realize that the more you level up, less fish you get. - You are now in the money grab zone. In order to get more fish, you need to level up your ingame skills. But to level said skills, you need to level up other skills. And to level up other skills, you need hundreds of hours and a lot of ingame money, OOORRR buy them with real money. - You decide to check you the cash store and notice that with the money you buy a few ingame coins (that you can barely buy stuff), you could buy most new games at full price because it's extremely expensive. And by the way, if you live in a country where the dollar is 5x more (like I do), you can't even suggest price localization to the devs, since they'll simply give you a rude answer and end the discussion.  In short, the devs should be arested for tricking people like this, and you should go play other fishing games.

>3000 hours on an account on the standalone launcher.  The game has practically ceased active development years ago. The most recent technical change that was visible and useful to the players was a crude implementation of a minimap in December 2024. Ancient bugs lay unfixed for years with multiple crucial mechanics broken in ways that directly impact gameplay, bug reports with clear repro steps are deleted and submitters banned from the forum.  Core gameplay elements of finding and catching fish are a complete black box with no way for the player to affect the outcome. The game uses the fishing spot mechanic, which, in practice, means that the rest of the map outside of those "hotspots" is virtually devoid of fish. You either fish with everyone else on the currently active spot or you're not fishing. The spots come and go at random intervals, meaning you regularly have to search the whole map again to find a new spot, or consult third-party resources on what's currently active. Spots are not guaranteed to be hot either, with situations where an entire waterbody is "dead" for weeks or even months being fairly common.  Individual biterate exists. Two players with identical progress in skills and skill point allocation using identical setups in the same spot going through the same motions will get wildly different results, far beyond what would reasonably be expected from "normal" RNG. One player could get multiple trophies in an hour, while the other would barely get any bites at all. There is no way to affect this behaviour from the player side, and your only hope is to be the lucky one today.  The game has one big plus going for it - the atmosphere. The visuals and music create a relaxing experience. If you want to unwind after a long day by rinsing some lures or watching a floater sway in the wind while occasionally getting [i]a[/i] fish out of the water while being content to restrict yourself to a few low-level maps - this game is for you. The gameplay is pretty much the same for players level 1 and 51, the only differences are the visuals of different maps and fish species, and the comfort afforded by top gear.  If you're a "power player" that wants to beat the game by getting that top gear, collecting trophies, beating the challenges or what have you, then you [i]will[/i] burn out. The grind is the worst I've ever come across in an MMO, and the completely obscured and half-broken mechanics that operate almost entirely on RNG prevent any experimentation or methodical practice that could improve your skill as a player. After enough time you'll realize that the only thing that matters is how the RNG is feeling right now, and that the only thing you could learn from all this is to let go.   I've seen players from the global top-100 with literally tens of thousands of hours invested since the release in 2017 just get up and leave, citing the stagnation at the current state of the game. It's not so much the fact that practically nothing positive is being done to the game per se, it's the fact that nothing is being done [i]while[/i] the game is fundamentally broken in so many ways. They'd be fully content to receive no updates with new maps or visual overhauls of old ones till the servers are turned off as long as the game's programming worked without bugs and in a way that made sense, and so would I.

- Tutorial lies about catch conditions, still no fish half the time.  - Too slow: nibbling forever, whole night with no fish, etc. It's not even a simulation, but has arcadey exp points, levels, unlocks, dopamine dings.  + Free, so people can try it.

Russian Fishing 4 promises a realistic fishing experience, but in practice it is more frustrating than relaxing. The mechanics are fiddly, the tutorials are lacking, and it can take far too long to figure out the basics. The fishing itself often feels repetitive, with very little variation between spots or fish types, which makes long sessions tedious.  The graphics are serviceable but not impressive, and performance can be inconsistent even on decent hardware. Loading times are longer than they need to be, and occasional bugs, such as fish not biting or lines getting stuck, break immersion entirely.  While there is a niche audience who may enjoy the slow pace and grind, for most players the experience is clunky and unrewarding. It feels like a game that is still trying to find its footing, and in its current state, it struggles to hold your attention for more than a couple of hours.  Final Verdict: 4 out of 10

This game demands Escape from Tarkov-level time investment and grinding before you can truly “have fun” and catch whatever fish you want.  The developers are lazy, arrogant, and show little care for Steam players. Don’t expect to transfer your Steam account progress to their standalone client for “extra benefits,” either. They’ll simply forget you ever asked—my friend tried and was left hanging, eventually giving up on the transfer ticket.  Russian and Chinese players are clearly favored. Their servers dominate global records, with rare trophies caught almost exclusively there. Seeing a world record from any other server is extremely rare.  Optimization is another weak point. For the visuals you get, performance is underwhelming. The graphics were designed with 4K resolution in mind, which causes shadows and especially fishing lines to look terrible on lower resolutions. There’s FXAA anti-aliasing support, but it barely makes a difference and often reduces perceived detail on fishing lines at lower resolutions.  TL;DR: The devs are lazy, the grind is brutal, and optimization is poor—but just like Tarkov, there’s no other fishing game quite like this. If you can overlook these issues and are willing to sink 1,500–2,000 hours into it, you will eventually enjoy it.

The game is far too grindy. Unfortunately, I've wasted over 100 hours of my life on this mess just to earn enough money from fishing to buy a mediocre reel that can still break if a larger fish gets hooked.  Be warned before you start playing: when the fish of a lifetime bites, there's always a chance it will get away. On top of that, expensive lures can be lost along with the fish if it snaps your line.  Progression is painfully slow, and it takes far too long to earn even a small amount of money for better equipment. The in-game shop prices are completely unrealistic. For example, if I wanted to buy a really good reel, I would have to spend around €50–€100, which is absolutely ridiculous.  Overall, the game relies heavily on grinding and pushes players toward spending real money if they want decent gear and reasonable progression.

After 4,000+ hours (this is my alt), I can say RF4 has the most absurd monetization I’ve ever seen. End-game gear runs $500+ for a single reel; mid-tier is $300—and you need three reels and three rods. Your choices: drop thousands of dollars, grind thousands of hours, or both.  The community is more toxic than that of Rust and the moderation? They don’t care. You can be banned for basic criticism, and appeals go nowhere. Security on the standalone (non-Steam) client is a joke, don’t even get me started.  The only reason this game has the player base it does is because it has zero real competitors. If another fishing sim shows up with mechanics even close to RF4, this game’s population will evaporate. People think EA or 2K are bad? FishSoft makes them look charitable. Even Star Citizen doesn’t touch these gear prices.  Verdict: Incredible fishing mechanics held hostage by a predatory pricing model, heavy-handed moderation, and a toxic community. If you value your time or your wallet, stay away.

After long consideration I've come to give this game a negative review, simply because not catching fish isn't fun. Now, physics and gameplay wise it's actually probably the best fishing game out there, however!  There are bad aspects, like REALLY bad, for example not catching fish... There is absolutely no mechanic or way to locate fish in this video game, you have to toss your hook in random places with random baits and see what happens. No jumping fish, splashing, ripples or bubbles indicating fish presence like in real or a video game that was thought through.  You will spend an eternity leveling up your character, and assigning skillpoints which you have to spend real money to refund, and being new to the game you WILL make mistakes as you wish you saved points or assigned them elsewhere.  You are required to level up at a snail's pace, just to use the most basic of basic fishing equipment and techniques. These items which can be purchased for in-game currency (which also will take literal years) to be able to gather enough for a good high level setup.  You are dependant on joining a discord channel to share locations and information which turn out to be unreliable, or simply expired and the fish have moved on. Try talking in-game and the community is just silent, there's no community here nobody will help you.  This game does not respect your time at all, and it will take you WEEKS to get real progression in this game. It also has crazy monetisation ($475) for a reel... an in-game fake reel.. that's for ONE rod and you will have many after a few weeks, and fish with 3 at a time. Imagine spending $1,425 on 3 reels to fish with in a video game..

Update: I’ve moved on from this game due to the "fish got away" mechanic, which happens due to a hidden state the developers refuse to disclose. To wait 10 minutes for a bite at a deep hole and reel for another 4 minutes only to see "fish got away" is super frustrating and very stressful. Meanwhile 100 gram mackerel bites lures that are 2x its size. oh yeah realism. I’ve uninstalled the game and moved on.  --------------------------------------------------------  Unrealistic game that heavily relies on BAD RNG.... im talking the worst and most unfair RNG you will experience in your life. You have to play for a year everyday in order to be able to afford an end game reel, and then another half year to afford a end game rod... or you can just pay them $400 and skip the grind of a setup... Leveling up is so bad... prices are bad, bite rate is garbage. there's hundreds of baits and lures, majority of them don't work, only a select meta few.   This game employs frustration like nothing else you've seen in your life and that so you pay money. I did, i payed a lot and i regret it, Don't pay them even your time... this game doesn't respect your time. Sitting for 3h only catching trash fish. Play Fishing Planet, it's a 100 times better more fun experience than this.

Its a great game I just wished the devs didn't blatantly favor the CN community. They enjoy an extremely boosted rate at rarer/trophy fish and have a heavily reduced cash store so the overwhelming majority runs the absolute best gear in the game. Yeah gear isn't everything, knowledge is i know. Its just for the example. This last big update with the water body reset is really highlighting the disparity in catches around the world.  If you have any doubt about the claim I invite you to dive down the rabbit hole, just google RF4 CN community and you'll probably land on a problematic event in the past. You can also use online webtools to access webpages that were scrubbed by the devs to help control the PR around the issues.   What cracks me up the absolute most is there are members in the CN communities who will post screen shots of their weekly keep net. Were talking like 4-6 hours in real life fishing in game, theyll post a keepnet with i kid you not 40+ eel trophies complaining that they never got a rare trophy. Anyone playing this game outside of that part of the world knows first hand that 40+ eel trophies in the span of 5-6 hours is straight up unheard of borderline impossible. The devs will say some bs like "oh they just share all their information with each other" Yes they do that, we also have the same access to that info they post and can do the same spot tests they do, EVERY single time you will get a tenth of the results they post at best.  Great game, just wished we all got to play the same game. New players, do not buy gold. Save your money for fishing gear in real life.

I’ve spent over 1,000 hours in this game on their standalone launcher. After accidentally purchasing the wrong premium membership, I requested a refund. They granted it — and then banned me.  According to their Terms of Use, refunding is not allowed. However, consumer law overrides such internal policies, which is likely why they processed it.  Still, they chose to punish me with a permanent ban — despite years of playtime.  I’ve never encountered a developer who penalizes users for exercising their legal rights.  If you make a mistake here, the consequences are harsh. I recommend extreme caution before investing time or money.

Another Pay-to-Win Disaster  Alright, so here’s the deal – yet another pay-to-win game that has absolutely nothing to do with real fishing. You put on a jig and the biggest hook, and you catch 60g crucian carp. You switch to the smallest hook, and suddenly you’re pulling in an 18kg cod. A five-day sea fishing trip? What a joke. It’s the same fish over and over, whether you use a black or red lure, small or big hooks—it doesn’t matter, the outcome is always the same.  For me, this is seriously the worst garbage I’ve played in years, and trust me, I’ve spent a lot of time in Fishing Planet.  @edit Under no circumstances should you buy premium. You spend money on premium, get 1 hour of happy hour, and what do you get? Barely 9 fish across 3 fishing spots, 6 of which are perch weighing only 60-120g... What a joke and a disgrace. A total waste of time, money, and nerves. Stay far away from this garbage.  The first sea expedition went okay, so I decided to go again to see what new things might be waiting for me... It was a failure. But, at my Twitch viewers’ request, I went out for the third time today... and it was a disaster. How can someone ruin a good experience like that?  Not only were there absolutely no fish — nothing at all was biting at 3–4 usual spots that always had some action — but today there was literally nothing. So, avoid those 5-day paid trips, but more importantly, don’t waste your money on premium — it’s really not worth it.

I think I just got handed an account ban in this game for playing it 10 hours in one day (this was also my first day playing it) and doing far better than the devs want me to. I DO NOT use any tools for tampering with games' memory pools; I also do not use macros of any kind that would take away the effort of performing routine operations like reeling the spinner line. I -do- use extra keyboard keys to rebind keys that are hard for me to reach comfortably with left hand, but in case of RF 4 the only key like that was Escape. All I did during my playthrough was put the game in borderless window, cast off my floater line and check how the floater is doing by just keeping a mouse cursor on the app icon on the taskbar while doing some other stuff, which surprisingly let me catch quite a LOT of fishies, enough to buy some new baits (fly was the most expensive one) and the simplest feeder rod.    I've no idea if the game monitors applications running in your RAM or if it's a case of paranoid anti-cheat system, but, in any case, before you decide to spend your money on premium currency - PLEASE KEEP IN MIND THAT YOU MIGHT GET BANNED FOR VIRTUALLY NO REASON

TLDR: Its a free to play game so obviously gameplay suffers to encourage "micro" transactions. Its also extremely slow and grindy again to sell "micro" transactions.  "Micro" is in quotations because a fishing reel and rod in game can cost as much as its real life equivalent in real money. Cons: -As a fishing simulator its unrealistic to encourage pay to win features. -As a game its extremely grindy with terrible rng to slow progress and encourage pay to win features. -The grind starts at level 1 where you will struggle to afford any basic gear and have no currency to upgrade, repair or experiment with different lures, baits, gear etc. -This is the kind of game that really leans into "the game gets good after 1000 hours"  -It took me around 12 hours irl playtime grinding efficiently to afford a "starter" set up  (After doing research and following guides/spreadsheets). -Skill system takes hundreds or if not thousands of hours to grind progress. -Basic fishing techniques, lures, baits, and simple equipment being locked behind a skill system that takes hundred of hours grinding menial tasks to unlock. -Decent fish are too rare prolonging the grind and reducing the excitement.  -Small fish dont respect hook or lure size. I really gave this game an honest try because I love fishing games and this looked promising.    But this is not a fishing game, it's a frustration simulator meant to extort you out of your money. Look, I get that F2P models need payments, I understand all that, BUT:  1. to increase in anything in this game it literally takes hundreds or thousands of tries at something, unless you have Premium. Even the Premium is a joke, you only have real-time hour in which the game treats you almost fairly. That costs 10 euros a month. Without premium, you only get XP at something maybe 1 out of 4 tries if you're lucky. This includes skill xp, level xp.  2. Everything costs incredibly much. Especially as a beginner, earning 100 silvers is often a big win. What does 100 silver get you? Not much. You will probably spend all that on something like Barley that you make over and over and over until you have 10k in your inventory and increase your skill by 0.1-0.2% per success. And these are beginner prices. More advanced things have prices going into the hundreds easily. The game gives the feeling that you need to earn to be able to earn more. That's what you do, you don't fish you grind.  3. Who ever bought hooks by the piece? Even Fishing Planet sells them by the pack, as does the rest of the universe. In RS4 a decent hook starts at 40 silver. For a single hook. Have a line break and lose it? Bye.  4. Who locks groundbait flavours behind skill levels that are insanely tough to reach? in my 30 hours playing I have about 15% groundbait skill. For that, I purchased well over a hundred of the basic flavor and just did that over and over.  5. There's no selling of gear. You just hoard it, god forbid the game actually give you 2 silvers for your old rod.  6. The fishing spots are exactly like in Fishing Planet, only difference is that they rotate between a few. Once you learn them, you just check them that's it. No wonder fish AI.  7. You have things like energy, comfort, hunger. Cool, immersive I guess. But their point is not to immerse you, it's to limit you. Shovel for worms to increase your skill? Nah, it takes all your energy to do once. Big fish, is it raining? Good luck with your energy. But there's things you can buy that will help.  8. You level up, you can allocate skills. Cool. Except that you get ONE free respec and then it's 5 euros per skill tree to respec. In 30h I am level 14. Basically any "wasted" skill will cost you heavily.  9. Stuff like, a 2kg fish will instantly surrender on the basic pole but catch it on a feeder rod? Be prepared for a fight. Esp early in the game.  10. Fish prices are sometimes wack. You'll see people farming 100 gram silverfish rather than big fish since by the numbers and esp if you get a few trophies, the payout for a bleak is better than for most early carp species.    Eh, let me say some Pros  - Graphics are nice. Nothing to write home about but not ugly either if you turn off the most ridiculously implemented fog ever. You can't see sh@# in front of you.  - The line physics is pretty good    That's it. Another ridiculous money grab game unapologetically elbowing you into spending a little something at every turn. And not cheap either.

TL;DR:  Don't play this game unless you are fine waiting long time for your targeted fish to become active or you are lottery addict which would make this game perfectly suited. It is hard to describe this game in short paragraph, but I will try. After almost 2k hours I do think I finally understood how game works and if you should play it entirely depends on what you expect from game. Game economy is very harsh so many players from start just follow best performing spots from discord or other places to reach their goals in terms of gear. Game doesn't make it obvious that such gameplay is all it can offer as finding active spots can be tricky and requires some luck, especially on lakes that are no longer that popular. On top of that game also doesn't guarantee even after all that wait that you will land trophy fish. Many experienced players report extensive amounts of gameplay invested to get for example koi trophy, not even rarest ones just regular koi on copper. It also explains why gear is so very expensive as player's churn rate is probably high once they have gear, but can't use it unless targeted species is active, which may take quite a lot of time, which is very bad from UX perspective, so by this approach they guarantee players do spend given amount of money before leaving as if you decide to grind it, you will probably spend on premium throughout the process. This mechanic also allows them to harvest more IRL money from players, for example if kaluga became active on rhinoceros larvae, you either need to have saved/farmed bait from earlier or spend IRL money to get bait and have a chance to land it.  Bottom line is if you do not want to keep an eye on discord and maybe even more places to find out what is active and fish that, game will not offer anything more, apart from few moments when stars align and you find active spot. But if you rather play in a way, oh man I want to fish cats/sturg/carp today, stay away from this game as it will not satisfy your needs. Better try Fisher Online, which is also grindy in terms of XP, but allows selective gameplay with moderate to hard mechanics. Man it feels great to just go fish whatever I want instead of waiting for "season" as we do in IRL fishing. That's what game should fill in, guess if you are not IRL fishing or can't IRL fishing, you may be somehow fine with this. If you decide to give this game try, just please do not sit without bites, if fish don't bite they won't start biting as it sometimes happen IRL.

THE ISSUE WITH THE GAME:   The game, as a fishing simulator, is great: mechanics, graphs, maps... they are pretty nice. When I have some free time, but I can't actually go fishing irl, I enjoy playing some hours of RF4. Well, I did, but not anymore: this potential is cut short by its business model, one of the worst I have experienced, even considering it is a f2p game.   EXPLAINING THE ISSUE:  1. PRICES MAKE THE GAME A MASSIVE GRIND   The amount of hours you need to invest in this game is absurd due to the difference between equipment prices and the money you get from fishes. I understand that certain grind and difficulty are necessary to keep the game interesting and absorbing, but RF4 is way, WAY beyond that point. The amount of hours you will need to invest into this game just to buy a decent piece of equipment is crazy, we are talking +100h. And then, a small mistake during a fight can render it completely useless; even repairs are absurdly expensive! Finally, if you land that big fish that can break your gear in an instant, don't think you have found gold... It will be worth <1% of the equipment cost AT BEST.   2. INVESTING REAL LIFE MONEY IS LUDICROUS   Maybe investing some real life money into the game can accelerate this? Not really. Equipment in the game is more expensive than the same quipment in real life BY FAR. I am not kidding, you can check. And then, you invest some money, cause you have played many hours and it is only fair. You buy some decent lures, cause the best equipment tipically can only be bought using real life money if you havent grinded every skill, and you start fishing with it. Suddenly, a fish bites, and even if you were fighting the fish perfectly, it can bite the line off. Just like that. Say goodbye to that lure and your money. I had this happened to me three times in less than two hours. Even premium is absurdly expensive and gives very little in return.   3. SKILL PROGRESS... YUP, ALSO MASSIVE GRIND  Do you want to take a guess about how you have to level up your non-fishing skills (like cooking, making groundbait, making lures, harvesting bait...)? That's right, you have to expend absurd amounts of money, which means massive amounts of time. You'll need to catch thousands (literally thousands) of fish to buy materials to craft thousands (literally tousands) of useless items (lures, groundbait and so on). So even if the game forces you to expend a crazy amount of time playing in order to make extremely slow progress, it is not a funny system of grinding. And by "not funny" I mean extremely repetitive and boring.   4. CASH-GRAB MODEL  So the only way to progress, since doing the same thing over and over again becomes boring eventually, remains to expend a ridiculous amount of money and countless hours just grinding. Overall, the whole game gives a vibe of anxious cash grabbing developers. You feel that, if you find a decent system (a set of spots and gear, for example) to make some money and progress that is a bit faster and funnier, developers will patch it up and ruin it. Why? So there are no short-cuts: they want you to invest thousands (again, literally thousands) of hours and dollars. And they are also extremely hostile to any criticism, I may even get perma-banned just for writing this here. This has happened to other negative reviews, you can just go and check.  In short, the game has the potential to be excellent, to be a game that I'd like to play for years, steadily investing some real life money here and there. But the business model is so awful, so hostile, so centered around sucking off your money and your time, that it is really not worth the time and frustration. I'd rather just grab my rods, take the car and go get frustrated with real fishing.

At first glance, Russian Fishing 4 offers an engaging and immersive experience, capturing the thrill of fishing with promising mechanics and deep skill-building opportunities. However, beneath the surface lies a game that eventually becomes an exhaustive grind, offering little to no meaningful rewards, even for the most dedicated players.  The Grind and Gear Progression While the early stages keep you hooked with progression, once you reach the point of aiming for top-end gear, progress slows to a near standstill. Expect to invest at least a year before acquiring the best equipment. And even when you finally reach this milestone, the reality is disheartening: catching trophy fish remains an exercise in frustration, with seemingly slim odds.  Competitiveness and Questionable Balance The experience becomes further soured by an overwhelming presence of top-performing players, particularly within certain demographics that seem to dominate the game's records with ease. The alleged lack of hidden mechanics in catch rates is dubious, as no substantial evidence has ever been provided to back these claims. Meanwhile, those spending real money on in-game currency appear to receive preferential outcomes, further tilting the odds in their favor.  Community and Moderation Issues Sadly, the game's community and moderation practices leave much to be desired. Discord moderation is plagued by power-hungry behavior, with bans dished out for trivial offenses such as using emojis in comments or challenging moderators. This has created an unwelcoming and toxic environment that discourages healthy discussion and engagement.  Development and Updates On the development side, progress and updates from the team are painfully slow. With one developer handling bug fixes every couple of months and meaningful updates coming at a glacial pace, the game fails to deliver new or exciting content that would justify its grind. High-level players receive no recognition or tangible rewards for their time and dedication, which only amplifies the monotonous experience.  Final Verdict To anyone considering picking up Russian Fishing 4, my advice is simple: save your time and money. The developers' greed and refusal to genuinely improve their game have rendered it a broken, imbalanced, and monotonous grind that fails to respect its players' time and efforts.  Fishsoft, your lackluster support, abysmal updates, and overall handling of this game speak volumes. Russian Fishing 4 is one to avoid.

Coming from FP I decided to give this game a try... after 30 hours I was able to come to a conclusion, at least from the FP comparison point IMO.  This game feels lonely! you never see anyone around ever! only your characters is visible on any map, where as on FP you get to see other players, what they're wearing, what level they are, what they caught!, this is a very important social aspect for me.  This game is grindy to death! you make such little income form fishes caught, even by completing the cafe order that it makes you wonder how many years it will take you to get to a higher level, I'm currently at level 13 and its been painfully slow! yes you can dish out money and get a boost, but Iwant to keep it F2P, like do with FP.  Forget about upgrading your gear for a long long time! prices are so out of the budget for 99% of the newer player base. they do this to force you to buy gold.  The skills and crafting options are very confusing, there are no tutorials that make it easier to understand, and if you look at FP, who wants to build their own lures and stuff? when you make enough money catching and selling fish to buy all the baits, takle and lures you can ever need.  The RPG aspects are unnecessary IMO, constantly having to eat, and go sit near the fire etc gets annoying, at least on a fishing game.  Lastly, every second in RL = 1 minute in the game, you in game (roughly) so a day lasts about 12 minutes, then it's night and you got  to run to the shelter to eat and warm up.   Bottom line is this, if I want to play the Sims, I'll play the sims, if I want play a fishing sim, I fire up FP, but RF4 tries too hard to be too much, has no good tutorial, is extremely grindy,has counter intuitive skills progression and mechanics, and feels like an offline single player game.

This game is truly peculiar. While other games aim for players to stay online for an extended period, in this game, the longer one remains online fishing, the more monotonous it becomes. I waited for three hours in reality, and when it got dark outside, there were no fish in the game, regardless of whether I changed the scene or searched for another location. Is this an online penalty? Punishing those who enjoy the game and desire to play it more frequently.  I am aware that fish movement, the allure of different baits, the application of various types of fishing tackle, etc. Spending two hours exploring different maps and the whereabouts of fish is insufficient. All I encounter are poor-quality fish and virtual fish that never materialize. So far, I have no game currency. I possess hundreds of fishing rods but have no currency because I am unable to catch fish. I have to constantly utilize one piece of equipment. I have no idea which equipment is effective, and I only have one opportunity for trial and error. New players will expend a considerable amount of time exploring equipment, but obtaining game money cannot sustain this process, creating a vicious circle. The more you attempt, the poorer you become. The poorer you are, the longer you spend, and the fewer fish you have. I simply enjoy fishing, yet there are no fish.

If you really enjoy trying to grind down stones using nothing but a silk shirt, you'll love this game. This is beyond the most "grindy" game I've ever played. You'll think you're doing well and all of a sudden the fish will stop once you reach a new level, forcing you to grind and grind to get the gear you need to go to the next unlocked water spot. If you try to go to the most recent water spot too early, without the right gear, you're going to break the gear you have. Then you have to find a way to grind and grind (usually with float fishing) until you can save enough to buy new gear or pay to get the gear fixed.   So if you have no life, and just want to hold a left mouse button for 10 hours a day for weeks, this game is for you, otherwise, don't bother.  This game needs some serious re-balancing.

Wonderful fishing game undermined by an extreme grind. This game was definitely the best fishing game I had played. Very realistic, a plethora of fish with different behaviors, gear galore, lovely graphics, and many different ways to fish. The game itself is enjoyable to play but, the grind is absolutely unbearable. Early game you will make quick progress. By the time you get to certain lakes you will not have the money or gear to fish even 50% of the species on the map. When you do start making decent money it will be reduced due to buying things like expensive boat licenses that last for five hours or less. Smaller fish are worth so little. You will catch many junk fish like perch, chub, and smaller carp species that will barely make you any silver. Perch will bite on hooks that are huge, in lakes with much larger fish. For some reason the prices on gear continue to go up. I imagine some of the later gear items take hundreds of hours just to get one reel and rod. I've played many grindy games like Runescape, WoW, Ffxiv, etc.. I've never quit one due to the grind. This will be the first. If you can accept that this game will soak up all your gaming time then I couldn't envision a more realistic game to fish on. For everybody else,  your time is better spent trying literally any other fishing game.

I have been an avid video game player for over three decades, and it's a rarity for me to encounter a game as grindy as RF4. While I must admit that the 3D models of the fish and gear, as well as the well-designed user interface, are commendable aspects of the game, unfortunately, they are the only positive aspects I can highlight.  As someone who has responsibilities in the real world such as family, work, and commuting, I found RF4 to be excessively time-consuming if I wanted to make any substantial progress without resorting to in-game purchases. The game demands an extensive investment of time, which clashes with the limited availability of free time for individuals like myself.  Moreover, unethical practices such as enticing players to purchase cheats and subsequently banning them, as recently reported, left me feeling deeply disappointed and disheartened. This, coupled with the constant and relentless increase in grinding requirements, ultimately led me to uninstall the game and seek solace in Fisher Online, a game that satisfies my fishing cravings without the excessive time commitment.  The frustration reached its peak during my attempts to fish in locations like Amber or Archipelago, where I would sometimes spend hours without catching any fish due to what felt like a disregard for the value of players' time. This deterrent can significantly impact the motivation of players to continue engaging with the game.  Another issue that left a sour taste in my mouth is the lack of transparency. For instance, the recent server update, which many suspect was aimed at making the grinding even more difficult, was met with disappointment and frustration.  Despite my extensive gameplay experience and the accumulated wealth across multiple accounts over the years, I never reached a point where I could afford three sets of Venga and Ti rods, essential tools to successfully pursue larger fish. From my perspective, this is an outrageous expectation.  Furthermore, the game's reliance on predetermined "spots" severely limits the fishing experience. If you don't fish in the same popular spots as everyone else, your success rate in catching fish diminishes. There is a noticeable absence of exploration and discovery when it comes to fishing spots in RF4.  I could continue expressing my grievances for hours, but I believe I have conveyed the essence of my dissatisfaction. In my humble opinion, RF4 is a subpar game that I strongly advise others to avoid. There are numerous other rewarding games available, and if you share my passion for fishing, I encourage you to give Fisher Online a try. It provides a decent fishing experience that actually delivers on the promise of encountering fish.  Wishing everyone a fantastic day and enjoyable gaming experiences with alternative titles.

I'm playing video games for around 30 plus years now and is very rare when I come across a game this grindy like RF4. The fish 3D models and the gear look good and also the UI is well done but those are really the only good parts. For someone who has family and work and commute and all the stuff anyone has to do in the real world, I found rf4 to be extremely time consuming if I want to make any progress in the game and I don't want to use the wallet. Unethical practices like luring players into buying cheats and banning them as it happened recently made me sick to my stomach. This and the continuous and relentless increase of grinding made me uninstall the game and completely move to Fisher Online just to satisfy my fishing sweet tooth. Going on Amber or Archipelago and not being able to catch any fish for hours sometimes because the developers are being so disrespectful towards the players time will deter a lot of players to continue playing. Another thing that got me sour is the lack of transparency, e.g. server update almost everyone knows now that is to make the grinding even harder. I literally have probably over 3k spanned across various accounts I have had over the years and even so I never got to the point to have enough money to buy 3 sets of Venga and Ti rods to properly be able to go after big fish. That is outrageous in my humble opinion. Another thing that I noticed is the "spots" based game style - you won't be able to catch fish at a decent rate IF if you don't fish at the same spots like everyone else. There is no fish spots discovery in this game whatsoever. I could continue for hours but I will stop here. Bad game, stay away from it. There are a ton of rewarding games out there and if you really are on fishing like I am, feel free to try Fisher Online, a decent fishing game that actually has fish in it. Have a great day everyone and have fun playing a different game :)

"Russian Fishing 4," as a fishing simulation game, should have provided a relaxing and enjoyable fishing experience. However, the game planners' actions of cheating, monopolizing resources, selling items, and limiting the number of fish caught per hour have severely damaged fairness and gameplay experience.  The planners' manipulation of resources makes it difficult for average players to enjoy the game and destroys equitable competition. The excessive sale of items overly commercializes the experience, exploiting player enthusiasm. The arbitrary limit on fishing undermines player freedom and the game's playability.  Furthermore, banning players on Steam for negative reviews or feedback highlights the planners' poor attitude towards criticism. These actions not only infringe on players' rights but also harm the development of a healthy game community.

This amendment expressly prohibits the government from making laws that abridge the freedom of speech. While private entities have more leeway in regulating speech on their platforms, the spirit of the First Amendment champions a broader cultural commitment to the free exchange of ideas. Thus, the game's practices not only raise questions about its respect for these ideals but also highlight a troubling disregard for the foundational value of free expression in a democratic society.

中国区发生了什么？来自一个玩家的简要说明  很抱歉打扰大家，我是来自中国地区的玩家，我只想解释一下在中国地区发生了什么。  “RF4老苏”是中国地区的游戏管理员。自2018年以来，他利用管理员的权利进行作弊而不受惩罚，垄断游戏设备，并禁止反对他的人使用游戏账号。几年来，中国玩家一直在向游戏官方报告，但没有得到有效的回应。“RF4老苏”仍然是游戏管理员。  最近，玩家们长期以来的愤怒爆发了，他们要求“RF4老苏”停止作弊，而“RF4老苏”则以禁止所有反对账号作为回应。很明显，这进一步激怒了游戏玩家，事件进一步升级。  短短几天内，数千名玩家的游戏账号被封禁，原因仅仅是他们对“RF4老苏”长期以来的作弊行为表示反对。   中国玩家感到非常失望，只能通过在STEAM上发布负面评价来表达对此事的不满。他们希望更高级别的游戏官员能够认真倾听他们的声音并积极回应。  但是由于语言不通，游戏官方认为中国玩家正在蓄意攻击游戏。于是，游戏官方已经禁止发布负面评论的用户使用游戏账号。  令人失望的是，2月29日，游戏官方人员Peter以视频方式回应了玩家，Peter并没有制止RF4老苏的犯规行为，而是认为中国地区的玩家正在发起一场“运动”，完全无视他们的合理要求，并强调发表差评会被封禁游戏账号。  与此同时，随着中国媒体对这一事件的报道，越来越多的其他玩家意识到了这一点，并对官方对游戏的处理表示不理解和愤怒。  仅在视频网站BILIBILI上，就有超过100万人观看了有关此事的视频，数以万计的用户通过留言表达了他们与钓鱼玩家相同的立场。  随着时间的推移，封号事件正在互联网上进一步蔓延。考虑到中国游戏用户数量已经达到6.64亿，无法预测这场活动最终会达到多大规模。  但是可以预见，在游戏官方对此做出积极回应之前，短期内，STEAM平台上对这款游戏的负面评价数量将继续增长。烂中烂  What happened in China? A brief description from a player  I'm sorry to disturb you. I'm a player from China, and I just want to explain what happened in China.  "RF4 Laosu" is a game administrator in China. Since 2018, he has used the administrator's power to cheat with impunity, monopolized the game equipment, and banned those who opposed him from using the game account. For several years, China players have been reporting to the game official, but they have not received an effective response. "RF4 Old Su" is still the game administrator.  Recently, players' long-standing anger broke out, and they demanded that "RF4 Laosu" stop cheating, while "RF4 Laosu" responded by banning all anti-accounts. Obviously, this further angered the gamers and the incident escalated.  In just a few days, thousands of players' game accounts were banned simply because they opposed the long-term cheating behavior of "RF4 Laosu".   China players are very disappointed and can only express their dissatisfaction by posting negative comments on STEAM. They hope that higher-level game officials can listen to their voices carefully and respond positively.  However, due to the language barrier, game officials believe that China players are deliberately attacking the game. As a result, the game official has banned users who post negative comments from using game accounts.  Disappointingly, on February 29th, the official of the game, Peter, responded to the players by video. Peter did not stop the foul behavior of RF4 Laosu, but thought that the players in China were launching a "movement", completely ignoring their reasonable demands, and stressed that publishing bad reviews would ban the game account.  At the same time, as the China media reported this incident, more and more other players realized this and expressed their incomprehension and anger at the official handling of the game.  On the video website BILIBILI alone, more than 1 million people watched the video about this matter, and tens of thousands of users expressed their same position as fishing players through messages.  With the passage of time, the title event is spreading further on the Internet. Considering that the number of game users in China has reached 664 million, it is impossible to predict the final scale of this activity.  However, it can be predicted that the number of negative comments on this game on the STEAM platform will continue to grow in the short term before the game official makes a positive response to this.  Что случилось в Китае? Краткое описание от одного игрока.  Извините за беспокойство, я игрок из Китая, и я просто хочу объяснить, что происходит в Китае.  «RF4 Lao Su» является игровым менеджером в Китае. С 2018 года он использовал права администратора для обмана безнаказанно, монополизировал игровое оборудование и запретил игровым аккаунтам против него. В течение нескольких лет китайские игроки сообщали об этом официально, но не получили эффективного ответа. «РФ4 Сью» остается игровым администратором.  Недавно вспыхнул гнев игроков, которые потребовали, чтобы RF4 прекратил мошенничество, а RF4 в ответ запретил все оппозиционные аккаунты. Очевидно, что это еще больше раздражает игроков, и инцидент еще больше эскалации.  Всего за несколько дней тысячи аккаунтов игроков были заблокированы только из-за того, что они выступали против мошенничества RF4.   Китайские игроки крайне разочарованы и могут только выразить свое недовольство этим инцидентом, опубликовав негативные отзывы на Steam. Они ожидают, что высокопоставленные игровые чиновники будут внимательно прислушиваться к их голосу и положительно отреагировать на них.  Но из-за непонятия языка официальные лица считают, что китайские игроки намеренно атакуют игру. В связи с этим, официальные лица запретили пользователям, размещающим негативные комментарии, использовать свои игровые аккаунты.  Разочаровывает тот факт, что 29 февраля официальный представитель игры Питер ответил игрокам в видео, который, вместо того, чтобы остановить нарушение RF4, считает, что игроки в Китае проводят «кампанию», полностью игнорируя их законные требования и подчеркивая, что публикация плохих комментариев заблокирует игровые аккаунты.  В то же время, по мере того, как китайские СМИ сообщали об этом инциденте, все больше и больше других игроков осознали это и выразили непонимание и возмущение по поводу официального обращения с игрой.  Только на видео-сайте BILIBILI видео посмотрели более миллиона человек, десятки тысяч пользователей высказали свою позицию по отношению к игрокам.  Со временем инциденты распространяются в Интернете. Учитывая, что количество пользователей игр в Китае достигло 664 миллионов, невозможно предсказать, насколько масштабная кампания в конечном итоге достигнет.  Но можно ожидать, что в ближайшее время количество негативных отзывов о игре на платформе STEAM будет продолжать расти, пока официальный ответ на этот вопрос не будет... cao

Player here with multiple account and > 4k hours.  Do NOT touch this game.  1. Economy is nonexistent, you will grind endless hours for levels, skills and equipment and nothing will change, only gives you an ability to catch "bigger" fish, the bite rate of the fish is horrendous, especially when we're talking about "good" fish. With every new release they make stocks limited, so if you're late you have no way to receive new items unless you pay cash or wait for 2 weeks. The amount of damage your gear takes and the repair costs vs the amount of silver you earn for that could make a grown man cry.  2. Premium - Will give you nothing other than experience boost, a bit bigger keep net, and submarine in Norway. Premium also allows you to send items to other players, but as a premium function, it's useless perk, since you can't send anything significant unless you pay for it with cash. Even without premium you're out leveling your gear faster than you can gather silver. So paying for premium is a double edged sword, you get to new maps faster but cannot afford the gear.. Open your wallet again.  3. Inflation - They randomly set prices of popular equipment higher, without increasing your ability to earn more.  4. Meta gaming - There is no experimentation in this game, well you can, but if you want to at least break even, you have to join some community( like discord, facebook, vk ) to find spots, baits etc.  5. You're pawn. Any critique you have to say, might get you banned, especially when you're writing your post out of frustration, admins are extremely sensitive. And even when you have anything good to suggest or critique it WILL NOT be heard, unless it's a money grab. How the game should work, you have absolutely no say in the matter.  6. There is not enough reward for progression, you just can go to new maps and catch bigger fish, but beyond some beginner maps, you'll earn 500 silver/h at any map on average, no matter how many hours you've spent or how good your gear is.  7. Game is developed in a way like they hate their player base and they want to get rid of them, it has been just patch after patch restrictions on how and how much fish you can catch. Back in the day you could fill 100 net with an hour or two, now few years later you're lucky to get 30 in an hour, unless you're catching complete trash. They've lowered average weight of fish over the years, so now you mostly catch tiny specimens. Game has in-game "quest" system that got reworked in a way that now restricts heavily how much extra silver you can earn from it. And adding more and more systems to get money out of your pocket.  8. Is your energy bill high? No problem, you have to have a game open while your repairs are in progress so you can pay even more.  9. Game has an extremely invasive anti-cheat, which will scan through your computer without your consent, so you can get banned for running running your everyday applications like autohotkey and many more undocumented list of applications (nobody really knows the depth of the list) and so forth.   10. Game gathers statistics and information off of your computer, including personal identifiable information without your consent. Even their crash handler will send full report of your PC to them without asking you, if your game ever happens to crash. All the information that has been gathered about you is being sent to Russia and is processed there.   Game is a bad, you should not invest any time into this, especially when it means you're putting your sensitive data to risk by doing that.  I saw a reddit point exposing such a matter too : https://www.reddit.com/r/Russian_Fishing_4/comments/1smmfun/this_game_is_doing_beyond_shady_stuff_with_your/  So play at your own risk.

I think this game has the best fundamentals for the fishing game, but it's just terrible at execution.  Developers really catched and created pretty real atmosphere of fishing. There are plenty o fish species, each one has different habits and actually bites differently. Each one also fights differently - this is very important for a fish game. You have authentic feeling, that you've gone fishing  But execution of game progress is just TERRIBLE. You are forced to spent countless hours (people have several thousand hours in this game) to actually get gear. There are many choices for rod and reels, but game FORCES you to actually buy several of them, because in-game currency is SO LIMITED, that you actually can't really experiment.  Also the problem with the game limitation of in-game currency for players created a spot meta-gaming, that actually you have to participate if you want to succeed in this game. And the funny thing is that developers seems really against sharing the spots - they want players to experiment in the game, but the economy they created forces players to actually limit experimentation to low as possible. Because you are punished by the game for experimentation. And this plays into microtransactions, because player frustration is creating incentive for buying the gear for real money (like 100-200 euro for a piece).  Which actually brings me to the next point - developers are acting like they're mad at the players :D This is really funny to see, but actually dev q&a posts are pretty agressive towards the players, that are using the game "not as intended". Which is also really childish.  This game lacks direction and is currently stagnating, and game developers are forcing their vision without actually working with the game and community. It seems like somebody from the outside plans features for this game.

As I am posting this i have left and abandoned Russian Fishing 4 for several reasons which I will explain.I already have about 275 hours spent in total on this game and have spent money on it multiple times.  Lately with the release of new small updates and patches Russian Fishing 4 has gotten very bland, boring and dead.This was a pretty sudden change in the game.Almost every single river/lake in the game makes me spend hours just to catch small little fish that are hardly even worth anything.To explain a lttle better i will wxplain all of the problems that I have experienced with Russian fishing 4: -Economy is horrible. The price of equipment is far too high when compared to that of the price of fish that you catch.Begginner rods that you would likely buy when starting the game cost around 200-ish coins BUT you also have to then save up for a reel that is also worth a similar amount of and on top of that there's also the fishing line,leader and tackle wich are around 60-ish coins.Now that we know the prices let's compare the money you receive for catching fish - 20-40 coins per irl HOUR on the begginner lake.And to make things worse if a big fish bites your bait it will likely break your rod or reel which, to repair is 60-70% of the price of the equipment. In conclusion you earn less than you should to be able to afford better gear. -For some reason FishSoft has decided to only focus on the new norwegian sea map making every other river/lake be almost completely dead.This means that you will likely spend 20-30 minutes on catching a SINGLE FISH. This change has made me furious as i had already spent real money on this game for a resaon.It's become way too boring of a game that it isn't even worth your time or money. -Skill progression is VERY SLOW. In the game you have to spend days maybe weeks to be able to buy a certain piece of equipment or use a different fishing method.It takes way too much time to unlock the most basic of skills like groundfishing for example.The game feels way too tormenting and un-rewarding and plain boring.  I want to add that Russian Fishing 4 has some very great potential at being a more hardcore fishing game.The problem is tha the developers are very lazy to fix even the easiest of issues.They are very sensitive to any critique and simply ignore their playerbase.FishSoft LLC has pretty much thrown this game in the dumpster by ruining it with crappy updates and patches.I want more RussianFishing 4 players and veterans to review bomb this game on steam on their forums so they would possibly listen and find out how bad the game really is.For now all we can do is post negative reviews and hope the developers change their minds for the better and finally fix the damn game.    These and various other obvious problems have caused me to no longer play Russian Fishing 4 and made my try some other fishing games.I would rather have this game be a paid game so it wouldn't be entirely foscused on grinding day and night and having to spend real money on the game.It's gotten to the point that it doesn't even feel relaxing anymore because in later levels Russian Fishing 4 will just drive you mad and make you impatient.I advise you do not try and waste your time on Russian Fishing 4 and try some other free fishing game alternatives like Fishing Planet or maybe paid games like Call of the Wild: The Angler.

Review from a 2600+hours player. level 42  Well, started as a great game, good graphics, real looking fishing experience. But over the few years, the game economy is going out of control due to what I think is being too "greedy"  Here are some of my opinions:  1. Being extremely grindy is completely fine, but it does not give sufficient amount of rewards vs time invested in the game. The core item costs way too much in real money and if you want to grind for particular end game item it will cost you weeks and months if not any less.  2. Zero trade -> absolutely no economy, unless you buy the gears in gold, which is real money. Silver(in game currency) gears are non-tradable and the cost of good silver items are just unthinkable.  3. Constant inflation on the good gears that people prefer. Tagara 10000 and Venga 10000 prices have been increased continuously, and guess what? expect further inflated price if this is how they manage the game.  4. Premium is always forced if you want to stay in the trend.  5. some fishing mechanism are left broken for years, and not effective at all to use them as grinding method. ex. some fishing skills such as texas rig, carolina rig, and so many others  6. skill stats are not transparent enough. It doesn't show how they exactly work and how much they will benefit your fishing experience. Investing in skill points on fishing skills have no general impacts. Groundbait skill and crafting skills definitely have noticeable boost from skill points.  7. There is a server discrimination between steam server and RF4's own standalone server. If you are on steam, you can't join competitions hosted by the standalone server. The servers are actually the same, you can talk people regardless of their choices on the server.

It's pay to win, where you get double xp for buying premium. Plus you also get a happy hour bonus where you can quadruple the xp you get. A year is over $100 and to do the lifetime its $2000. Why a lifetime is even an option at that amount I have no clue as the server will be dead and gone before you get your money's worth. If you buy a shorter period, as that is more economical, if the servers go down then you are out of luck and seems to happen too frequently. For the amount of money premium cost and the amount of premium users there shouldn't be as much down time nor the amount of lag there is with the servers. Just means they are pocketing more money than spending on servers/equipment/maintenance. Baits, equipment, reels, rods and other stuff are locked behind real money. So at some point you have no choice but to spend money to progress. It's hard enough when a bottom tier of rod is over $10 and the fish you get are worth $.01-$.15 unless you get something big. At a higher tier lake an average over 100 fish (2-3hrs) was about $.30. Which may seem decent until you realize that you can afford the pole but now the reel is $50+ and line is $10+ and the lure starts around $20+ with good low end being $50+. Don't snag a weed or break a line as you will also have to replace that again if you can afford to. I have snagged randomly while fishing with no reason to be snagged and lost a decent amount of money due to it. You can go multiple in-game days with catching less than 20 fish even with good gear and fishing technique.  The above is only low tier entry level stuff. I'm level 13 in 37 hours with premium (mistake) and looking at progression it only gets worse and bolsters the premium requirement. There are rods and reels that are $40,000+ and yes that is forty thousand dollars plus. As a newb in Norwegian sea in a 3 hour trip I was able to make $500. This is the best fishing area and the gear they provide you with which is low tier like you would normally start with and you will need 26+ hours at that rate to get JUST the top of the line rod. So add the reel which will double that. This doesn't include any breakage or equipment wear that will be incurred as well as the amount of food that you need to eat to maintain energy to keep fishing. Throw in any travel charges as it costs you money to travel between locations when you get bored or want to fish for other species which increases as the location gets better. I would note the pricing for locations but go figure, servers are down wasting premium time that you cant recoup.   With all that being said its not a bad game to put you to sleep before bed time as long as you don't get hooked, no pun intended, into spending all of your real money on it!

Very intuitive game if you already know the fishing basics or are a real angler. Its a long and immersive game with great locations, great gameplay, many fish species, lots of equipment, and more realistic mechanics than the few other games available out there - Unfortunately, this uniqueness comes with a bittersweet ripoff sensation that, as you progress thro levels, the game gradually turns into either a money pit or a time consuming bait:  - Fishing techniques and equipment availability depends on skill progression, which is painfully slow - Youll be doing the same thing over, and over, and over, and over again, before you can simply change from a spinner to a spoon or choose a different sinker setup. - The reward you get for caught fish VS the price of equipment is ridiculously disproportionate - You need to sell hundreds of fish just to afford a medium quality reel.  This eventually leads you into spending a few bucks to speed things up, problem is a few bucks wont get you very far in this game. The most expensive fishing line costs around 840 silver coins, which is 8 Gold coins, which is almost 15,00 USD - just for a line.  So theres that, its a great game where sooner or later you get stuck in between having to invest a crazy amount of time or ridiculous money just to gain access to new stuff and come out of routine.

Of course you'd say that, EVERYONE would say that.  But when you're a morally bankrupt studio, one that designs a horrible anti-cheat program, you don't do that. You accuse the player of lying, you absolutely REFUSE to prove your claim, and you move on as if you're some kind of "hero" for falsely banning someone. Their claim is that it was not, and that I'm simply lying about the "other" program, the "other" program they refuse to even name. How very interesting.  Not once, not one single time in my life have I cheated in a video game, and I've played thousands starting in the mid 80's. I even played this game for 73 hours, with Reshade installed (you know, the program that falsely triggered their anti-cheat, which they refuse to admit), with no problems.  Be warned, this company WILL ABSOLUTELY ban your account with a false claim of cheating attached. I've never experienced this with a game in my life. The game looks great, beautiful worlds, water looks great and player animations is good. HOWEVER fish mechanics isn't that good, Sometimes it is like the fish got stuck and just sits there in the water like it was programmed that way to simulate a struggling fish, I can still live with that HOWEVER making silver(Game Currency is a massive grind. Fishing equiptment is insanely expensive. the shops to buy fishing stuff is really unrealistic and stupid. you go to tackle store to buy worms and it says out of stock! How can a line of code that makes up that product be out of stock. that means instead of buying 10 worms for 8.95 you now have to buy 300 worms for 179 (prices are for arguments sake only) You could buy Gold with real life money and buy stuff with gold but the prices are INSANE!. Levelling up takes forever and ever and ever. Unless you buy premium wich doubles XP earned and once a day for an hour(Happy Hour) tripples Xp earned. YEs the game is free to play butif you dont want to spend insane totals of real life money you could spend days, weeks even months catching fish between 1 and 500 Gram. It is the best game out there in my opinion but A GRIND TO THE BONE or IT BREAKS YOUR BANK! It is a very bittersweet experience!
